attacking_style: Leverages time trial power for explosive accelerations in breakaway selections and uphill finishes. Capable of animating early moves despite TT specialty, particularly on difficult uphill finales where sustained power advantage emerges.
team_tactics: Deployed as insurance rider in early breakaways when team's climbing tactics uncertain. Used flexibly—not confined to TT days—to cover moves or gain time on parcours favoring sustained power.
terrain_preference: Difficult uphill finishes where time trial power translates to gains. Struggles on pure flat TT parcours compared to expectations. Second-half/late-race efforts appear stronger than opening efforts.
vulnerability: Form deficits exposed on expected-strength parcours (flat TTs). Loses time in first half of stages, suggesting pacing/warm-up issues or early-race energy management weakness.
key_patterns: Time trial power explicitly used for breakaway aggression and selection, not just TT stages (2 instances)
Demonstrates second-half/late-race power advantage, losing time early then gaining back (2 instances)
Animated early breakaways despite TT specialty when deployed tactically by team (2 instances)
Underperforms expectations on flat TT parcours relative to credentials and titles (1-2 instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: Mixed signals—2023 showed late-race power gains, but 2022 revealed form deficit on expected-strength terrain. Inconsistent application of evident physical strengths.
